title: WIP - code coverage
body:
Work in progress to get code coverage into the CI.

Unit tests are not sufficient as most code is tested via the e2e tests (and the system tests).

Working:
* unit tests
* system tests

comments.body: One step further: e2e tests can now run with coverage as well. Maybe needs more massaging until they turn green but I am optimistic.
What's going on here? I just pushed...
Eeeek. I don't see it merged in master, which is good.
Nice...integration are green now. Only system tests are going south. So close...

> `Δ = absolute <relative> (impact)`, `ø = not affected`, `? = missing data`
> Powered by [Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/containers/podman/pull/7065?src=pr&el=footer). Last update [da00482...34d4083](https://codecov.io/gh/containers/podman/pull/7065?src=pr&el=lastupdated). Read the [comment docs](https://docs.codecov.io/docs/pull-request-comments).

I gave this a good try: perl/awk with `seen` hashing is much slower than `sort -u` (like, 70s vs 45s for ~1k cover files). I tried perusing the files manually to see if my eye could detect useful patterns for a custom tool. No luck. This is a pretty hard problem.

> I gave this a good try: perl/awk with `seen` hashing is much slower than `sort -u` (like, 70s vs 45s for ~1k cover files). I tried perusing the files manually to see if my eye could detect useful patterns for a custom tool. No luck. This is a pretty hard problem.

Thanks a lot for checking, Ed, I appreciate it!

The last remaining idea I have is to not fully "divide and conquer" but to stop at a certain threshold. Instead of merging _N_ profiles into 1, we could merge _N_ profiles into _M_ and upload them. The upload script supports that and Codecov is apparently smart enough to combine multiple uploads on the server side.

I guess the crux is that we don't need to find the optimum. We're doing a `sort -u` to reduce redundancy and hence trim down the size. AFAIK Codecov has a size limit of 100 MB per uploaded file. We could short cut with something similar to a `sort -u --until=100MB` where we're sorting until we hit the specified threshold.

Simple to code in a high-level language but I didn't find a way with coreutils.

@edsantiago, I regenerated (and removed) the token. I also wrote a small go script which merges the profiles in 26 secs on my machine:
```
libpod (test-coverage) $ time go run hack/coverage/coverage.go -dir ~/profiles -profile coverage.txt
Merged 6776 profiles (11.6 GB) into "coverage.txt" (3.5 MB)

real    0m25.975s
user    0m25.246s
sys     0m4.490s
```
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
Closing for now. It's a massive amount of work and I don't see it getting a higher priority in the near future.
